step_by_step_thinking:
The non-radiographic methods for determining root canal working lengths include using digital tactile sensation to feel the end of the tooth, assessing apical periodontal tissue sensitivity as an indicator, or utilizing electronic apex locators that measure resistance. Since option D encompasses all these techniques, it is the most comprehensive answer.

Answer: D To address this question comprehensively, we need to consider multiple non-invasive methods available clinicians can use without relying on traditional X-rays. These include both subjective tactile sensations through digital palpation and objective measurements provided by electronic devices like apex locators. Given this range of options, "All of the above" best captures the full spectrum of non-radiographic approaches currently employed.
